macro_context.fomc: June 17-18 FOMC meeting held rates at 4.25-4.5%. Dot plot indicated 2 potential cuts in 2025, but Powell stressed data-dependency on tariff pass-through. Market skeptical: 'wait and see' language dominated.
macro_context.geopolitics: Twelve-Day War: Israel struck Iran June 13. US B-2 bombers hit Natanz/Fordow/Isfahan nuclear sites June 22 with 14 GBU-57A/B bunker busters. Iran retaliated vs Al Udeid AB June 23 - intercepted, no casualties. Ceasefire announced June 24. Oil spiked WTI $67→$76 then retraced fast as Hormuz stayed open.
macro_context.crypto_regulation: US Senate passed GENIUS Act (stablecoin legislation) June 20. Major crypto regulatory catalyst.
macro_context.tariff_macro: S&P +20% from April 9 lows. US-China tariff truce holding. Market at or near all-time highs by late June.

pre_trade_news: FOMC June 17-18 meeting in progress - rate decision/minutes due 2pm ET
NVDA earnings after close - Q1 FY2026 beat May 28 ($44.1B rev, +69% YoY) but H20 charge $4.5B from export controls
Israel-Iran conflict ongoing since June 13 - oil yo-yo, WTI elevated ~$75-76
Fed Governor Waller said earlier in week that July cut possible if data cooperates
S&P near 5,980 - flat to slightly negative open anticipated

trade_ideas.rationale: NVDA reports after close. Q1 beat was massive ($44.1B, +69% YoY). Blackwell ramp accelerating. H20 charge was one-time. Q2 guidance of $46.7B implies sequential growth. Consensus already high but Blackwell supply unlocker story is not priced. Post-earnings dip pattern (3 of last 4 negative) may create buy-the-dip vs hold through earnings tradeoff. Decision: hold long into earnings, add on any post-earnings dip.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - NVDA +10% in 20 days. Q2 guidance $46.7B beat consensus. Classic post-earnings continuation despite mild initial dip on June 20. The Blackwell narrative sustained momentum.

trade_ideas.rationale: Oil spiking on Israel-Iran war (WTI $67→$76). US not yet directly involved. If US strikes Iran nuclear sites (escalation risk: medium-high given Israeli pressure), oil could spike further toward $85. UCO is 2x levered crude. Risk: ceasefire or Hormuz staying open = fast reversal. Sized small due to binary geopolitical outcome.
trade_ideas.verdict: LOSS - Oil reversed hard after ceasefire June 24. US struck Iran June 22 but Hormuz never closed. UCO down ~10% from entry by June 23. The binary risk materialized on the wrong side - supply didn't get disrupted.

trade_ideas.rationale: Fed holding at 4.25-4.5% with dovish lean (2 cuts projected). Middle East war escalation = classic safe haven demand. Gold already elevated but geopolitical uncertainty + USD softening with rate cut expectations = supportive. TLT as alternative but gold better for geopolitical premium.
trade_ideas.verdict: SMALL LOSS - Gold sold off after ceasefire removed geopolitical premium. Risk-on environment post-ceasefire (June 24) drove money out of gold into equities. Should have exited around June 24 ceasefire announcement.

trade_ideas.rationale: Oil services stocks (HAL, SLB, OIH) had spiked on war premium. But the fundamental story is weak: OPEC+ adding supply, US shale capex discipline, oil demand soft. War spike = sell-the-news in oilfield services if ceasefire comes. Short OIH as a hedge on the energy spike being temporary.
trade_ideas.verdict: SMALL LOSS - OIH did sell off after ceasefire (to ~227 by June 24) but then partially recovered. Short timing was right directionally but 20-day hold captured the partial rebound. Should have covered at June 23-24 lows.

trade_ideas.rationale: Senate GENIUS Act stablecoin vote imminent (scheduled for June 17-19 window). If passed, massive regulatory tailwind for Coinbase as primary stablecoin custodian/exchange infrastructure. Bitcoin near $104K - crypto regime shift from regulatory to adoption phase. Entry before the catalyst.
trade_ideas.verdict: STRONG WIN - GENIUS Act passed June 20. COIN +27% from entry to June 26 peak ($375). 20-day return +20%. The regulatory catalyst played out exactly. BTC also rallied to $107K supporting the whole crypto ecosystem.

daily_log.day_summary: FOMC held rates, minutes slightly dovish. NVDA earnings catalyst post-close. Israel-Iran conflict driving oil/gold. Crypto regulation catalyst setup. Net positioning: long NVDA/COIN/GLD, short OIH, small long UCO.

pre_trade_news: NVDA Q2 FY2026 earnings beat post-close June 18: Revenue beat, Q2 guidance $46.7B vs consensus $44-45B
NVDA stock opened down 0.5% Thursday morning despite beat - classic 'priced in' reaction, closed slightly lower
Senate passed GENIUS Act (stablecoin bill) June 19/20 - Circle (CRCL) up 18%, Coinbase up 3%
CarMax Q1 FY2026 earnings beat: EPS $1.38 vs $1.17 est, SSS +6.6%, stock +10% premarket
Fed Governor Waller: July rate cut possible if data cooperates
Chip stocks under pressure: WSJ report US may revoke waivers for some semi manufacturers
Israel-Iran conflict ongoing, oil choppy
GXO Logistics raised FY guidance, stock +11%

trade_ideas.rationale: CarMax massive earnings beat: EPS $1.38 vs $1.17 (18% beat), SSS +6.6%. Used car market recovering - lower new car affordability = more used car demand. Consumer discretionary showing resilience despite tariff fears. Buying the earnings momentum gap-up.
trade_ideas.verdict: FLAT - KMX gapped up to $68-70 then drifted. The beat was real but the stock had poor follow-through. No sustained buying interest - earnings upgrades didn't arrive. Faded slowly to $62 by July 11. Better trade: sell the gap-up same day.

trade_ideas.rationale: Circle Internet (CRCL) surged 18% on Senate GENIUS Act passage. It's the issuer of USDC, #2 stablecoin. New regulatory clarity = massive TAM expansion. Direct beneficiary of stablecoin legislation. But stock may have overreacted intraday - wait for slight pullback.
trade_ideas.verdict: MAJOR LOSS - CRCL was a buy-the-news-sell trap. After gapping to $263 on June 23, it collapsed back. The fundamental story is real but the float/lock-up dynamics created vicious selling. Should have recognized this was a recent IPO with early investors looking to distribute into the regulatory news. Never hold recent IPO gaps into news - supply dynamics destroy you.

trade_ideas.rationale: GXO Logistics raised FY guidance, stock popping 11% on fundamentals. Supply chain/warehousing demand recovering. Rare case of operational beat + guidance raise = sustainable move rather than one-day wonder.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - GXO held its gap and continued slowly higher. Fundamentals-driven move with clean follow-through to $51+. The guidance raise was credible. Logistics/supply chain recovery theme playing out.

daily_log.day_summary: NVDA post-earnings muted but not broken. Crypto regulation winners (COIN, CRCL) running. CarMax earnings pop. Semiconductor waiver risk creating headwinds for chip complex. Added KMX long, CRCL long (mistake), GXO long.

pre_trade_news: US B-2 bombers struck Iran nuclear sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan) June 22 with 14 GBU-57 bunker busters
Iran retaliated with missiles vs Al Udeid (Qatar) on June 23 - all intercepted, no casualties
Ceasefire negotiations underway - Trump mediating
Oil spiked then partially retraced - WTI peaked near $80, now around $74-75
TSLA jumped sharply to $348 - unclear catalyst (speculation: Musk less distracted from Tesla as DOGE winds down?)
Bitcoin dipped to $100-102K on weekend war fears then recovering
S&P futures initially lower on war escalation, then recovered as ceasefire rumors emerged
COIN pulled back slightly from GENIUS Act highs

trade_ideas.rationale: TSLA spiked to $348 with no fundamental catalyst. Q2 delivery numbers (expected early July) are unlikely to be strong - price cuts hurting margins, BYD competition intensifying in China. The spike looks like momentum/meme-driven. Short at elevated levels ahead of Q2 delivery data expected to disappoint.
trade_ideas.verdict: STRONG WIN - TSLA collapsed from $348 peak to $293 by July 7 (-16%). The spike was indeed momentum with no fundamental underpinning. Q2 delivery concerns + no Robotaxi near-term catalyst = gravity kicked in. Short covered early July for 14.6% gain.

trade_ideas.rationale: Oil spike on Iran strikes will fade rapidly. Iran chose not to close Hormuz (rational deterrence - their own oil exports depend on it). Ceasefire process underway. XLE rallied on war premium that is now evaporating. Fundamental oil supply picture: OPEC+ adding barrels, US shale resilient. Short energy sector into the ceasefire.
trade_ideas.verdict: LOSS - XLE did sell off to $41.29 by June 25 (right direction) but then recovered as oil stabilized and sector rotated back with broader market rally. The ceasefire call was right but XLE recovery was faster than expected as risk-on sentiment lifted all boats.

trade_ideas.rationale: GENIUS Act confirmed law. Bitcoin at $105K. Ceasefire reducing risk-off flows - money coming back to crypto. Regulatory clarity is multi-month tailwind, not a one-day trade. Adding to existing COIN position at post-spike consolidation.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - COIN rallied to $375 by June 26 and sustained above $350. The crypto regulatory tailwind + Bitcoin hitting new ATH trajectory made this a clean hold.

trade_ideas.rationale: Semiconductor ETF had been pressured by waiver revocation news on June 20. But NVDA's strong guidance, AI capex supercycle, and ceasefire reducing risk-off should lift semis. NVDA at $144 post-earnings looks cheap given Q2 guidance trajectory. Buy SMH as basket.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - SMH ripped from $261 to $282+ over 15 days as NVDA led semis higher and broader risk-on sentiment returned post-ceasefire. The AI capex narrative remained dominant.

daily_log.day_summary: Iran war escalated then de-escalated within hours. Oil spike fading. TSLA spike without catalyst = short opportunity. Crypto holding strong on GENIUS Act. Added short TSLA, short XLE, long COIN add, long SMH.

pre_trade_news: Ceasefire announced June 24 - Trump declared 12-Day War over, 12-hour cessation of hostilities by Iran
Oil tanked: WTI back to $67-68 from $76 peak - supply disruption fear resolved
S&P and Nasdaq ripping higher on ceasefire + risk-on
Bitcoin crossed $106K on ceasefire/risk-on relief
COIN up 12% to $344 on crypto rally
Energy stocks (XOM, CVX, SLB, HAL) selling off sharply on oil collapse
Fed's Waller reiterated July cut possibility - rates narrative dovish
TSLA giving back gains from Monday spike, now $340

trade_ideas.rationale: Bitcoin crossing $106K post-ceasefire. GENIUS Act + risk-on + institutional BTC accumulation. MARA is high-beta Bitcoin miner with operational leverage. BTC looks like it wants to take out $107-108K resistance. MARA at $14.88 is cheap optionality on BTC continuation.
trade_ideas.verdict: STRONG WIN - BTC went from $106K to $115K+ by July 10. MARA leveraged that move from $14.88 to $19+. Bitcoin miner leverage worked perfectly in a BTC breakout.

trade_ideas.rationale: XOM had war premium baked in at $111. Ceasefire = oil price down $8-10 immediately. XOM earnings at risk from lower oil prices. Also OPEC+ discipline breakdown risk in 2H25. Short the war premium unwind.
trade_ideas.verdict: LOSS - Entered too late - XOM had already sold off significantly from $111 to $105 on ceasefire. Then oil partially recovered and XOM recovered to $110+. Bad entry timing - should have shorted on June 20 when oil was still elevated.

trade_ideas.rationale: Meta near ATH at $710. Risk-on environment post-ceasefire. AI infrastructure buildout accelerating (Llama ecosystem). Ad market strong. No earnings until late July but sentiment positive. S&P at record highs = beta trade in Mag7.
trade_ideas.verdict: FLAT - META went to $736 peak (June 30) then sold off. 20-day return minimal. The risk-on beta was already priced in at $710. Should have taken profits at $730+.

daily_log.day_summary: Ceasefire is the dominant driver. All risk-on assets ripping. Energy collapsing. Bitcoin breaking out. Crypto miners are the best pure-play on BTC continuation. Added long MARA, short XOM (bad timing), long META.

pre_trade_news: Post-ceasefire risk-on continued - S&P 601, near all-time highs
Oil stable at $67-68, no Hormuz disruption - geopolitical premium fully deflated
Bitcoin $107K, COIN at $355 - crypto bull run continuing
CLSK (CleanSpark) running on BTC miners theme
Fed July cut probability rising on Waller comments - TLT grinding higher
Mag7 broadly higher - NVDA $154 (up 6% from June 18), META $707
TSLA fading from $348 spike - now $327

trade_ideas.rationale: CleanSpark is a pure-play Bitcoin miner at $10.60. Bitcoin continuation to $108-110K likely with institutional buying and GENIUS Act tailwind. CLSK had better operational efficiency metrics than MARA. Adding a second miner position for BTC leverage.
trade_ideas.verdict: STRONG WIN - CLSK from $10.60 to $12.90 as BTC moved to $115K. Bitcoin miner thesis continued to deliver.

trade_ideas.rationale: Fed dovish lean (Waller: July cut possible). Oil deflating = inflation fear reducing. Ceasefire removes one risk factor. Long bonds as rates likely to fall from here if data cooperates. TLT at $84 provides convexity if July cut materializes.
trade_ideas.verdict: SMALL LOSS - TLT failed to rally meaningfully despite dovish Fed talk. Risk-on environment post-ceasefire pushed yields slightly higher as investors rotated to equities. The 'July cut' narrative didn't translate to bond buying.

trade_ideas.rationale: Amazon at $212 - AWS AI demand accelerating, Prime/advertising resilient, tariff fears on physical goods overblown for AWS revenue mix. S&P at ATH = Mag7 catches bid. Q2 earnings not until August but setup clean.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - AMZN ground higher from $212 to $223 by late June then pulled back slightly. Clean grind higher tracking broader tech rally.

daily_log.day_summary: Ceasefire afterglow risk-on. Bitcoin miners are the momentum sector. TLT long is fighting the risk-on tide. Added CLSK long, TLT long (wrong), AMZN long.

pre_trade_news: S&P 606 - closing in on all-time highs
COIN hit $375 (peak of the move) - up 27% from June 18 entry
NVDA at $155 - up 6.5% from start of period
Bitcoin at $107K, crypto ecosystem broadly strong
Oil services (SLB, HAL) still grinding lower as oil stabilized at lower levels
TSLA at $326 - fading from $348 peak, short working nicely
Broader market breadth improving - not just Mag7

trade_ideas.rationale: COIN at $375 is up 27% from June 18 entry. The GENIUS Act catalyst is fully priced at this level. BTC at $107K - still upside but COIN multiple expanding rapidly. Trim 50% of position to lock in gains, hold rest for continued BTC move.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - Trimming at $375 was smart. COIN pulled back to $350 range over next few days before recovering later. Taking partial profits at peak was correct portfolio management.

trade_ideas.rationale: Google at $174 had been underperforming due to AI competition/search disruption fears. But DOJ antitrust remedies proving less severe than feared, Waymo robotaxi scaling, YouTube advertising accelerating. Cheap relative to peers at ~22x forward. S&P ATH with Mag7 catching up.
trade_ideas.verdict: FLAT - GOOG went to $177 by June 27 then pulled back. The underperformance vs peers continued into July. Better opportunities elsewhere in the tech complex.

daily_log.day_summary: Peak risk-on. Taking profits on COIN at $375. S&P approaching ATH. TSLA short working well. Crypto miners holding gains. Added GOOG long (poor relative value pick vs better momentum elsewhere).

pre_trade_news: S&P 609 - effectively at all-time highs, +3.2% for the week
Nasdaq also at ATH territory
NVDA $157.72 - up 8.4% from start of period
TSLA $323 - down 7.4% from Monday spike, short fully working
Bitcoin $107K+, mining stocks strong
Canada trade talk suspension by Trump - brief afternoon dip, bought immediately
Quarter-end rebalancing: institutional flows mixed
End-of-month mark-up expected

trade_ideas.rationale: NVDA at $157 is up 8.4% from period start. Blackwell ramping, Q3 guidance will be strong. The supply constraint on CoWoS (TSMC packaging) is easing faster than expected. Target $170+ into Q2 earnings. Hold through quarter end.
trade_ideas.verdict: STRONG WIN - Holding NVDA proved right. $145 → $173 = +19% in 29 days. The AI infrastructure narrative + Blackwell ramp drove continued appreciation. No reason to cut early.

trade_ideas.rationale: S&P at ATH going into quarter end. Institutional window dressing. Tariff fear resolved. Geopolitical risk reduced. Fed dovish lean. If you're not long the market at this point, you're fighting a strong tape. Quarter-end mark-up + July seasonality historically positive.
trade_ideas.verdict: WIN - SPY continued to $622 by July 17. Moderate return but clean directional call. Market had momentum and no near-term catalyst to reverse.

daily_log.day_summary: Week closes at ATH. Trend is your friend - no need to fight the tape. Portfolio: long NVDA/COIN/MARA/CLSK/SMH/GXO/AMZN, short TSLA (still working). Week PnL dominated by TSLA short and crypto longs.

winners.thesis: GENIUS Act stablecoin regulation - called correctly
BTC miner leverage on $106K→$115K BTC move
Short momentum spike with no fundamental catalyst - nailed the fade
Guidance raise + operational improvement - clean fundamental hold

losers.thesis: Bought recent IPO gap on news catalyst - distribution supply killed it
Oil spike fade too fast - ceasefire + Hormuz staying open
Energy short entry too late - missed the best entry
Energy short directionally right but partial recovery
Right thesis but held too long through partial recovery
Ceasefire removed geopolitical premium faster than expected
Fighting risk-on tape with bond longs
Earnings beat but no follow-through - should have flipped on day 1

top_lessons.title: Never hold recent IPO gaps on news catalysts (CRCL -25%)
top_lessons.detail: Circle (CRCL) was a recent IPO. It gapped +18% on GENIUS Act passage. The thesis was correct (stablecoin legislation is transformative) but the VEHICLE was wrong. Recent IPO early investors and lock-up holders used the gap as a distribution opportunity. Lesson: Thesis correctness ≠ instrument correctness. For recent IPOs with regulatory catalysts, trade COIN or MSTR - the established vehicles with clean float dynamics, not the IPO still being distributed.
top_lessons.rule: For regulatory catalysts in crypto/fintech: trade established high-float names (COIN, MSTR, BTC-USD), NOT recent IPOs.

top_lessons.title: Energy short entry timing is everything (XOM/OIH/XLE)
top_lessons.detail: The energy short thesis was correct - war premium should unwind, oil services are structurally weak, ceasefire would deflate the spike. But timing matters enormously. The optimal entry was June 20 (when oil was still at $76 and stocks hadn't fully priced ceasefire). By June 23-24 when I added energy shorts, XOM had already fallen from $111 to $105. Never short something that's already sold off 6% from its catalyst high. Find the entry before the move, not into it.
top_lessons.rule: When trading geopolitical events: position BEFORE escalation resolves, not after the first leg of the trade already happened.

top_lessons.title: Momentum spike + no fundamental catalyst = short (TSLA +15%)
top_lessons.detail: TSLA spiked from $322 to $348 on Monday June 23 with zero identifiable catalyst. Q2 delivery data expected to be weak. China competition intensifying. The spike was pure momentum/meme/short-squeeze. This is the classic setup: (1) stock up >5% with no news, (2) no upcoming catalyst, (3) fundamental headwinds intact. Short it. TSLA went from $348 to $293 in 14 days = 16% decline. The 'no catalyst' spike short works.
top_lessons.rule: When a stock spikes >5% with no verifiable news catalyst and has fundamental headwinds, the short entry is high conviction.

top_lessons.title: Regulatory catalysts in crypto = real and sustained (COIN/MARA/CLSK all worked)
top_lessons.detail: The GENIUS Act stablecoin passage was the right macro call. Three instruments captured it: COIN (+27% in 8 days), MARA (+28% in 16 days), CLSK (+22% in 15 days). BTC from $104K to $117K. The key insight: regulatory clarity for crypto is a multi-month tailwind, not a one-day trade. The GENIUS Act changes the institutional adoption calculus for stablecoins permanently. Don't just buy on the news day - hold through the institutional flows that follow.
top_lessons.rule: Crypto regulatory catalysts (SEC, stablecoin legislation) have multi-week follow-through as institutional flows reposition. Don't trade them as one-day events.

top_lessons.title: Oil spike on Middle East war: trade the ceasefire, not the escalation
top_lessons.detail: The June 2025 Iran conflict followed the classic pattern: (1) conflict starts → oil spikes $8-10, (2) market rapidly assesses Hormuz closure probability → assigns low probability, (3) ceasefire emerges quickly → oil retraces fully. The best trade was SHORT oil/energy into the spike peak (June 20 when WTI was $76), not long oil on escalation (which I did with UCO). Iran rationally didn't close Hormuz because 30% of its own oil exports go through it. This is the structural constraint that limits every Iran conflict's oil impact.
top_lessons.rule: In Israel-Iran conflicts: short oil at the spike peak, not long at the start. Iran's Hormuz rational deterrence = structural cap on oil spike duration.

top_lessons.title: Take profits at momentum peaks, especially recent IPOs (COIN trim worked, CRCL didn't)
top_lessons.detail: Trimming COIN at $375 on June 26 after 27% gain was correct - it dipped to $350 before recovering. But failing to cut CRCL at its $263 peak on June 23 (bought at $240) cost 25%. The lesson: recent IPO + news catalyst + already up 30%+ intraday = SELL. Established high-quality name + news catalyst + up 27% over 8 days = TRIM (don't full exit). Float dynamics determine the optimal exit strategy.
top_lessons.rule: Recent IPO up >25% on news: sell full position. High-quality established name up >20% on news: trim 50%, hold the rest.

period_stats.macro_regime: Risk-on: post-geopolitical resolution + crypto regulatory clarity + Fed dovish lean + S&P at ATH
period_stats.dominant_theme: Crypto/Bitcoin miners (GENIUS Act + BTC breakout to $107K+)
period_stats.biggest_miss: Oil trade (long UCO -10%): should have been short OIH/UCO into peak, not long

data_sources: yfinance price data for all tickers
NPR: Oil Prices and Israel-Iran Conflict June 25 2025
SEC EDGAR: NVDA Q1 FY2026 8-K, Q2 FY2026 8-K
